questions: Locate the official Mac User Guide landing page that features dedicated sections for 'Apple Intelligence', 'Switched from Windows to Mac?', and 'Control your privacy and security'. Based on the 'macOS Support' resources typically associated with this guide, identify the specific recommended tool or procedure for: (1) regaining access if an Apple ID password is forgotten, (2) reinstalling the operating system without removing personal data, (3) automatically backing up data to an external storage device, and (4) keeping the Mac up to date.

rounds.question: Locate the official Mac User Guide landing page that features dedicated sections for 'Apple Intelligence', 'Switched from Windows to Mac?', and 'Control your privacy and security'. Based on the 'macOS Support' resources typically associated with this guide, identify the specific recommended tool or procedure for: (1) regaining access if an Apple ID password is forgotten, (2) reinstalling the operating system without removing personal data, (3) automatically backing up data to an external storage device, and (4) keeping the Mac up to date.
checklist_width: Procedure: Reset Apple ID password (for forgotten passwords)
Tool: macOS Recovery (for reinstalling without data loss)
Tool: Time Machine (for automatic backups to external storage)
Action: Upgrade macOS / Keep your Mac up to date
checklist_depth: Target: Mac User Guide (containing 'Apple Intelligence' and 'Privacy' sections)
Context: macOS Support topics (distinguishing 'Reset Password' from 'Recover Deleted Password')
rounds.rationale: The question uses 'Deep' logic by filtering for the specific user guide structure (mentioning 'Apple Intelligence' and 'Windows Switching') to identify the correct context. It then applies 'Wide' logic by requiring the aggregation of four distinct support procedures (Password, Reinstall, Backup, Update) found in the associated support list, ensuring the agent retrieves multiple scattered facts.
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: macOS Support Reset your Apple ID password If you forgot your Apple ID password, you can reset it and regain access to your account. Learn how to reset your password Reinstall
rounds.answer_a:
The official Mac User Guide landing page that contains dedicated sections for 'Apple Intelligence', 'Switched from Windows to Mac?', and 'Control your privacy and security' is the guide for macOS Sequoia [1]. Based on a review of Apple's official macOS support documentation, the recommended procedures for the specified tasks are as follows:

1.  **Regaining Apple ID Access:** If you forget your Apple ID password, the recommended procedure is to visit Apple's account recovery website, iforgot.apple.com. This tool guides you through the process of resetting your password using a trusted device or an associated email address or phone number [2].

2.  **Reinstalling macOS without Data Deletion:** To reinstall the operating system while preserving personal data, the correct procedure is to use macOS Recovery. By starting up your Mac in Recovery Mode and choosing the "Reinstall macOS" option, the OS is installed over the current system, leaving user files and settings unharmed [3].

3.  **Automatic Backup to External Storage:** The designated tool for automatic backups to an external device is Time Machine. After connecting an external drive and setting it up as a backup disk, Time Machine works in the background to automatically create periodic, incremental backups of your entire system, including personal files, apps, and settings [4].

4.  **Keeping the Mac Up to Date:** The official method for keeping macOS current is through the Software Update feature. This is located in System Settings (Apple menu > System Settings > General > Software Update). Here, you can check for, download, and install the latest available macOS updates and security responses [5].

## References
[1] Apple Inc. (2024). *Mac User Guide for macOS Sequoia*. Apple Support. Retrieved from support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/welcome/mac.
[2] Apple Inc. (2024). *If you forgot your Apple ID password*. Apple Support. Retrieved from support.apple.com/en-us/HT201487.
[3] Apple Inc. (2024). *How to reinstall macOS*. Apple Support. Retrieved from support.apple.com/en-us/HT204904.
[4] Apple Inc. (2024). *Back up your Mac with Time Machine*. Apple Support. Retrieved from support.apple.com/en-us/HT201250.
[5] Apple Inc. (2024). *Update macOS on Mac*. Apple Support. Retrieved from support.apple.com/en-us/HT201541.
rounds.answer_b:
The official Mac User Guide landing page is at Apple's support site, featuring sections on **Apple Intelligence**, **Switched from Windows to Mac?**, and **Control your privacy and security**.[5]

From associated **macOS Support** resources:

1. **Regain access if Apple ID password forgotten**: Use account recovery via iforgot.apple.com or the Apple Support app; reset with trusted device, phone number, or recovery key if two-factor authentication enabled.[5][6]

2. **Reinstall macOS without removing personal data**: Use **Startup Disk** in Recovery Mode (Command-R at startup) to reinstall via internet recovery or macOS Reinstaller, preserving user data and apps.[5]

3. **Automatically back up to external storage**: Enable **Time Machine** in System Settings > General > Time Machine; select external drive and set to back up automatically.[5]

4. **Keep Mac up to date**: Go to **System Settings > General > Software Update** for automatic checks and installs; enable "Keep your Mac up to date" for seamless updates.[5][6]

These procedures ensure data safety and system integrity per official guidance.

## References
[1] https://support.apple.com/guide/macbook-air/mac-user-guide-apdfdad69354/mac  
[5] https://support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/welcome/mac  
[6] https://support.apple.com/guide/mac-help/get-started-with-your-mac-mchl3a2c2cb0/mac
rounds.judge_reasoning:
Agent A is superior in both accuracy and presentation. 

1. **Deep Logic/Context**: Agent A correctly identifies that the presence of 'Apple Intelligence' points specifically to the **macOS Sequoia** User Guide. 

2. **Procedural Accuracy**: For Task 2 (Reinstalling), Agent A correctly identifies the tool as the 'Reinstall macOS' option within Recovery. Agent B hallucinates/confuses terminology, suggesting the use of 'Startup Disk' (which is for selecting a boot volume) or a non-existent 'macOS Reinstaller'. 

3. **Formatting & Citations**: Agent A provides a professional reference list with titles and dates, whereas Agent B only lists raw URLs. Agent A's explanations are also clearer and more precise.
